run.head: 790f597bedaa92c1aa8b7db956e34fb4ae8722f4
gaps: The read-only filesystem prevented cargo test, cargo clippy, ignored browser tests, force-red mutation, and Radius impact-map generation; cargo fmt --check and git diff --check passed.
The gate script encountered temporary-file permission errors, so its surfaces, siblings, callers, and producers results were independently checked by reading and repository-wide searches.
Network access was unavailable for reopening the external Playwright, WAI-ARIA, HTML-ARIA, and Chromium sources named by the getrole checklist.

lenses.name: Inverse regression surface
lenses.reason: The locator-miss classifier was narrowed and reordered.

lenses.name: New-domain matrix
lenses.reason: Fill values now flow through text, alt, and title locator arms, and early validation reaches every find-family command.

lenses.name: Resolution-rule consistency across consumers
lenses.reason: No overlapping hostname, route, or key resolution rule changed.

lenses.name: Emission channel and one-shot latch reachability
lenses.reason: No warning channel or one-shot latch was added.

lenses.name: Shim hermeticity
lenses.reason: No external-binary test shim was added.

lenses.name: Deliberate-default check
lenses.reason: The removed find actions were advertised but never implemented, rather than working defaults.

lenses.name: Fresh-seam scan
lenses.reason: No runtime or framework was patched to expose a new signal.

lenses.name: Reference-implementation oracle
lenses.reason: The PR validates and exposes getByRole dispatch, triggering the complete getrole contract checklist.

lenses.name: New-failure-outcome propagation
lenses.reason: Find-family commands now return an early unsupported-action error before launch and locator resolution.

lenses.name: Error-path forcing
lenses.reason: The diff adds validation, classifier, WebDriver translation, sentinel, and fallback branches.

lenses.name: Non-destructive recovery
lenses.reason: No heuristic recovery action was added.

lenses.name: Cancellation and timeout hygiene
lenses.reason: No asynchronous wait, timeout, or cancellation mechanism was added.

lenses.name: Boundary pipeline trace
lenses.reason: Find behavior crosses CLI parsing, daemon dispatch, MCP translation, documentation, and the Eve TypeScript extension.

lenses.name: Substrate verification
lenses.reason: New ignored tests infer action success from command responses and therefore require substrate-level inspection.

lenses.name: Docs-behavior parity
lenses.reason: The accepted action set, fill-value support, help, MCP schema, docs, and Eve capability advertisements changed.

lenses.name: Demonstrative example
lenses.reason: The placeholder examples changed from type to fill and a role text example was added.

lenses.name: Complexity budget
lenses.reason: No new lookup or membership check runs per DOM element or locator candidate.

deterministic.reason: Added lines contain neither prohibited em dashes nor double-hyphen comment punctuation.
All required README, docs, skill, MCP, and help surfaces are present in the diff.
The retired click/fill/type/hover/focus/check/uncheck/text list has zero repository hits.
The two remaining hits quote the historical failure in comments and are not executable contract values.
Manual repository-wide passage inspection replaced the script result because here-document temporary files were denied; stale exact-match claims were found and reported.

Manual call-site inspection replaced the script result; element_id_from_value has one caller in the changed WebDriver client, while validate_find_subaction is intentionally called at execute_command entry.
Manual producer enumeration replaced the script result and covered Chrome/CDP locator messages, WebDriver no-such-element payloads, semantic locators, nth, frame resolution, and multiselect.
The filesystem prevented reverting fixes and compiling the added regression tests, so their claimed red state could not be demonstrated.
The branch diff has no whitespace errors.
Cargo could not create target temporary files in the read-only workspace.
Cargo could not create target temporary files in the read-only workspace.

findings.claim: find role does not implement implicit-role or AX-to-ARIA normalization, including the no-ARIA-spelling RootWebArea to document mapping.
findings.evidence: handle_getbyrole constructs document.querySelectorAll('[role="{role}"], {role}'). Consequently heading searches only explicit role="heading" or a nonstandard <heading> element instead of <h1>-<h6>, and document searches never translate Chromium RootWebArea to the public ARIA role document.

findings.claim: find role ignores ordered fallback semantics and mishandles none/presentation conflict resolution.
findings.evidence: The exact CSS attribute selector cannot resolve the first supported token in role="button none" or role="none button". It also matches role="none" and role="presentation" literally without excluding focusable elements, including tabindex="-1", or elements carrying global ARIA properties, while no code distinguishes global attributes from non-global attributes such as aria-level.

findings.claim: Role accessible-name matching is case-sensitive by default and does not compute the accessible name.
findings.evidence: The default branch uses case-sensitive String.includes against only aria-label or textContent. It omits aria-labelledby and other accessible-name sources, while the reference contract requires case-insensitive substring matching by default.

findings.claim: The CLI parser rejects documented optional-action forms such as find role button --name Submit and find text SignIn --exact.
findings.evidence: parse_find always assigns rest[2] to subaction before parsing flags from index 3. Therefore --name or --exact in the optional action position becomes the subaction and the new early validator rejects it as an unknown action, despite usage strings showing [action] followed by those options.

findings.claim: Every updated public surface still overstates --exact as a general exact text match instead of scoping it to each locator's actual matching field and, for roles, the accessible-name filter.
findings.evidence: README.md:207, cli/src/output.rs:2072, docs/src/app/commands/page.mdx:107, and the Eve schema all say exact text match. For role queries, exact changes only the optional name comparison and never the role token; placeholder, alt, title, and testid paths do not consult exact at all.

findings.claim: The newly added ignored find-action test does not prove the advertised actions affect the intended DOM substrate.
findings.evidence: e2e_execute_subaction_dispatches_every_find_action checks only response success. In particular, fill never reads the input value and check never reads checked state. Browser execution was unavailable, so the end-to-end substrate behavior could not be forced.

findings.claim: The newly advertised fill capability for text, alt, and title drops its value before daemon dispatch.
findings.evidence: Each changed parse_find arm writes fill_value into cmd["value"], MCP call_find and the Eve extension append the value argument, and execute_subaction reads cmd["value"] for fill.

findings.claim: find role returns the first match rather than enforcing Playwright-style strictness when multiple elements match.
findings.evidence: handle_getbyrole returns immediately on the first matching element and contains no ambiguity check.

exemptions.claim: The two remaining Unknown subaction text hits are exempt from stale-value removal.
exemptions.evidence: Both hits are historical explanatory comments; executable errors now use Unknown action with the valid action list.

exemptions.claim: docs/src/app/selectors/page.mdx is exempt from restating the accepted find action list.
exemptions.evidence: Its semantic-locator section contains examples only and makes no completeness claim about supported actions.

exemptions.claim: The WebDriver malformed-payload messages are exempt from locator-miss guidance.
exemptions.evidence: No element in response and No element ID in response represent missing protocol fields, while genuine no-such-element payloads are translated to the anchored locator-miss form.

issue_candidates.title: Decide whether role locators should use strict-mode or first-match semantics
issue_candidates.evidence: handle_getbyrole marks and returns the first matching element without detecting multiple matches.
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: The house norm classifies strict-mode versus first-match as a genuine product decision rather than a correctness defect.
